sound_type: shrill twitter -- rapid, high-pitched, trilling vocalization at nocturnal colony. Eerie chorus of multiple birds in darkness. E=4
ecosystem: Island colonies, typically predator-free islands. Breeds in burrows. Feeds at sea. Nocturnal at colony.
season: Summer breeder. Nocturnal at colony. At sea year-round outside breeding.

coordinates.description: Ancient Murrelet on island colony. Haida Gwaii (Queen Charlotte Islands) -- major breeding population. Also visits PNW waters.

taxonomy_note.description: SIXTH ALCIDAE (174-179). Streak closes. Six consecutive entries. Six nesting strategies. Precocial chick departure (1-3 days). Named for gray 'elder's shawl' plumage.
taxonomy_note.family_count_in_engine: Alcidae: Pigeon Guillemot (174), Rhinoceros Auklet (175), Tufted Puffin (176), Common Murre (177), Marbled Murrelet (178), Ancient Murrelet (179).
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Synthliboramphus from Greek 'synthlibomai' (compressed) + 'ramphos' (bill) -- compressed bill. antiquus from Latin 'antiquus' (ancient) -- gray plumage resembling elder. Murrelet: diminutive of murre.
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Synthliboramphus -- 'ancient' murrelets. ~6 species. Small Alcidae. Precocial chicks. Nocturnal colonies.
taxonomy_note.order_note: Charadriiformes. Alcidae concluding streak from degrees 174-178. Sixth and final consecutive entry.
taxonomy_note.family_position: Sixth Alcidae. First Synthliboramphus. Streak closes. Most precocial chick departure in Alcidae.

song_metrics.song_duration_s: Individual twitter burst: 0.5-2.0 seconds. Colony sound continuous at night.
song_metrics.note_structure: Rapid, high-pitched, shrill twittering. Trilling quality. Multiple notes per burst. Sharp.
song_metrics.note_types: Colony twitter (primary -- nocturnal), parental call (chick departure guidance), alarm.
song_metrics.phrase_structure: Rapid trilling bursts. Colony: overlapping twitterings from multiple birds creating shimmering chorus.
song_metrics.timbre: Shrill, high-pitched, rapid. Trilling. Eerie in nocturnal context.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=4. Penetrating but not loud. Colony aggregate atmospheric. Nocturnal context amplifies eeriness.
song_metrics.tempo: Very rapid within individual twitter bursts. Colony tempo continuous.
song_metrics.context: Nocturnal colony activity. Island breeding sites. Contact, arrival, chick departure guidance.
song_metrics.key_property: SHRILL TWITTER at nocturnal colony. Chicks depart at 2 days old. 'Ancient' gray plumage. SIXTH ALCIDAE -- streak closes. E=4. v1.49.319.

plumage.overall: Black head with fine white streaks. Gray back (the 'ancient' shawl). White underparts. Short dark bill. Small, compact seabird.
plumage.diagnostic_mark: Gray back (elder's shawl). White head streaks. Black crown. Short bill. Compact body.
plumage.seasonal_variation: Minimal. Breeding and non-breeding similar. White streaks may be more prominent in breeding.
plumage.flight_pattern: Fast, direct, low over water. Rapid wingbeats. Small wings. Nocturnal at colony.

physical_description.overall: Length: 24-27 cm. Weight: 180-230g. Bill: short, compressed. Small, compact.
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: Gray 'ancient' back. White head streaks. Short compressed bill. Nocturnal at colony.
physical_description.field_id: Small compact seabird with gray back and white head streaks. Nocturnal at colony -- rarely seen on land during day. At sea: look for gray-backed small auk.

nodes.title: Two-Day Departure -- Most Precocial Seabird
nodes.description: Chicks leave nest burrow at 1-3 days old. Never eaten in burrow. Cannot fly. 25-30g. Scramble to sea at night guided by parental calls. Family swims away -- potentially hundreds of km.

nodes.title: Nocturnal Colony -- Shrill Twitter in Darkness
nodes.description: Colony active only at night. Shrill twittering, rapid trilling creates eerie nocturnal soundscape. Anti-predator strategy. E=4 through contextual eeriness.

nodes.title: The 'Ancient' Name -- Elder's Gray Shawl
nodes.description: Gray back resembling old person's shawl. White head streaks like gray hair. *antiquus* = ancient. Understated, dignified plumage among flashy Alcidae.

nodes.title: Alcidae Streak Closes (174-179)
nodes.description: Six consecutive Alcidae entries. Six nesting strategies. Longest family streak in the engine (ties Scolopacidae at 5, exceeds it at 6). The auks complete.

cross_references.relationship: MURRELET PAIR! Marbled (178) and Ancient (179) in sequence. Two murrelets, different genera. Forest nester vs island burrower. Both mysterious.
CHICK DEPARTURE CONTRAST! Murre: 15-25 days. Ancient Murrelet: 1-3 days. Both dramatic departures but murrelet is far more precocial.
ALCIDAE STREAK! Guillemot opens (174). Ancient Murrelet closes (179). Six species. Six strategies. Complete family survey.
TAXONOMIC REVOLUTION! After 6 consecutive Alcidae (birds), degree 180 enters MARINE MAMMALS. The Alcidae streak bridges to the mammalian ocean.
